mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer spending, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving S&P 500 equity prices up over a short-to-medium term horizon.
failure_condition: The S&P 500's 20-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with the month-over-month percentage change in RSAFS over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a weakening labor market, which typically precedes a slowdown in overall economic activity and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 40% of instances where the UNRATE trigger condition is met, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is high and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is also elevated, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy environment aimed at curbing inflation. This typically dampens economic growth and corporate valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 30% of periods where both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS meet their respective high thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: In a period of strong economic growth, robust housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong consumer and business confidence, which translates to positive short-term market sentiment and S&P 500 equity returns as future demand is signaled.
candidate_trigger: HOUST reports above 1500 (thousands of units) for two consecutive months.
failure_condition: During confirmed economic expansion regimes, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances where HOUST is above the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy manufacturing and supply-side activity, indicating strong underlying economic momentum that supports corporate earnings and thus equity valuations over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward returns of the S&P 500 do not exhibit a statistically significant positive correlation with the month-over-month growth of INDPRO over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration or contraction in non-farm payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth signals a weakening labor market, which typically leads to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, negatively impacting S&P 500 equity prices.
candidate_trigger: The 3-month average change in PAYEMS is negative.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 35% of periods where the 3-month average change in PAYEMS is negative.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is slowing significantly while the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) remains high, it indicates a stagflationary or recessionary environment where monetary policy is tight despite weakening demand, leading to prolonged negative S&P 500 equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 25% of periods where both GDPC1 growth is low and FEDFUNDS is high.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation environment, a sudden and significant drop in the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a potential policy error or an attempt to stimulate a rapidly weakening economy, which can cause short-term market uncertainty and negative S&P 500 equity reactions.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS drops by more than 0.5% in a single month AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 3%.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where FEDFUNDS drops significantly during a period of high inflation.
